introSection: Caterpillar is a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer D.P.V. Rides currently located at Lightwater Valley in Ripon, Yorkshire, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride opened in 2003 as the Caterpillar and was located on the spot of the former Batflyer. For the 2014 season, the ride was relocated within the park, rethemed and renamed Little Dipper. For the 2021 season, the ride was relocated and repainted in the park. In 2023, the ride was renamed back to Caterpillar.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride follows one of the most cloned layouts ever, the Big Apple / Wacky Worm figure-eight layout.

fullText:
Caterpillar is a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer D.P.V. Rides currently located at Lightwater Valley in Ripon, Yorkshire, England, UK.

The ride opened in 2003 as the Caterpillar and was located on the spot of the former Batflyer. For the 2014 season, the ride was relocated within the park, rethemed and renamed Little Dipper. For the 2021 season, the ride was relocated and repainted in the park. In 2023, the ride was renamed back to Caterpillar.

The ride follows one of the most cloned layouts ever, the Big Apple / Wacky Worm figure-eight layout.
